text:
Ethan Park: quick review request on scaling policy update endpoint. main open items: validation rules + RBAC matrix. want to lock this before we merge pr-48252.

Benji Okafor: 👍 dropping the current API shape here (from the PR) so we can nit:

Benji Okafor: ```http
PATCH /v1/dedicated/deployments/{deployment_id}/scaling-policy
Content-Type: application/json

{
 "min_replicas": 4,
 "max_replicas": 20,
 "scale_up_cooldown_seconds": 60,
 "scale_down_cooldown_seconds": 300,
 "predictive": {
 "enabled": true,
 "mode": "bias_reactive",
 "max_scale_up_step": 4
 }
}
```
Benji Okafor: response 200 returns effective_policy + warnings array (strings). errors are 400 w/ code.

Jordan Lee: on validation: we should be explicit on partial updates. if a customer PATCHes just `max_replicas`, we validate it against existing `min_replicas` (effective), not default min=0.

Chloe Martin: +1. console will...

title: #eng-platform: 1747600311
preview:
Ethan Park: Need to lock down where the KMS client wrapper lives in the monorepo + what the API looks like. We’ve got 6+ call sites across control-plane + a couple in data-plane. If we don’t standardize now it’s going to drift again.

Benji Okafor: +1. I’m about to touch control-plane secrets service and I don’t wan...
text:
Ethan Park: Need to lock down where the KMS client wrapper lives in the monorepo + what the API looks like. We’ve got 6+ call sites across control-plane + a couple in data-plane. If we don’t standardize now it’s going to drift again.

Benji Okafor: +1. I’m about to touch control-plane secrets service and I don’t want to pick a random folder and regret it.

Jordan Lee: Security ask is basically: one wrapper, one error taxonomy, no bespoke retry loops. Location matters because we want it hard to bypass.

Diego Santos: Proposal: `packages/security/kms/` (or `libs/security/kms/` depending on our conventions). It’s a cross-cutting dependency but it’s security-sensitive.

Vikram Singh: Data-plane question: does runtime also import from that path? Some of our data-plane services are in `services/inference/*` and we try to keep deps minimal.

Ethan Park: Yeah, ideally both planes use the same...

title: #eng-platform: 1747160501
preview:
Ethan Park: starting a thread on HubSpot auth for the signal router service — OAuth scopes, token rotation, secret storage, audit trail requirements. we need to lock this before we merge the integration PRs.

David Kim: :wave: from HubSpot side: we should use a HubSpot public app w/ OAuth (not a private app token). ...
text:
Ethan Park: starting a thread on HubSpot auth for the signal router service — OAuth scopes, token rotation, secret storage, audit trail requirements. we need to lock this before we merge the integration PRs.

David Kim: :wave: from HubSpot side: we should use a HubSpot public app w/ OAuth (not a private app token). private app tokens are easy but auditing + rotation is worse and security is going to hate it.

Marcus Lin: +1. private app tokens are basically long-lived bearer secrets. OAuth w/ refresh tokens is the right default. also want least-privilege scopes.

Ethan Park: ok. current plan in PR uses OAuth refresh token stored in Secrets Manager, access token cached in memory w/ TTL. question is: which scopes are actually required for MVP? we’re doing:
- company lookup by domain / name
- upsert company properties (custom properties)
- create timeline note/activity
- create task assi...

title: #eng-platform: 1747118830
preview:
Ethan Park: trying to close the loop on KMS config validation. where do we enforce what?

- control plane API (create/update deployment config)
- private installer preflight
- runtime/data-plane startup

goal: catch misconfig early + actionable errors, but also avoid breaking existing installs w/ stricter checks. th...
text:
Ethan Park: trying to close the loop on KMS config validation. where do we enforce what?

- control plane API (create/update deployment config)
- private installer preflight
- runtime/data-plane startup

goal: catch misconfig early + actionable errors, but also avoid breaking existing installs w/ stricter checks. thoughts?

Benji Okafor: +1. my take: we need *layers*.

1) control plane should reject obviously bad configs (missing required fields, invalid ARN format, region mismatch, impossible combos like keyId empty + kms enabled)
2) installer preflight is best place for permissions checks for Private (since it has the customer’s creds + knows the env)
3) runtime should still do a “last line of defense” check but shouldn’t be the first time we tell someone their IAM is wrong

Jordan Lee: agree on layers. question: what’s “obviously bad” vs “permission bad”?

format validation is chea...

title: #eng-platform: 1746989001
preview:
Logan Wright: seeing some weirdness in staging private cluster: log forwarder (vector) CPU pegged + memory creeping, then we start dropping logs. anyone else seeing backpressure?

Diego Santos: yep. noticed `vector` pod restarted twice overnight. grafana panel: `container_memory_working_set_bytes` hits limit then OO...
text:
Logan Wright: seeing some weirdness in staging private cluster: log forwarder (vector) CPU pegged + memory creeping, then we start dropping logs. anyone else seeing backpressure?

Diego Santos: yep. noticed `vector` pod restarted twice overnight. grafana panel: `container_memory_working_set_bytes` hits limit then OOMKilled.

Rafael Mendes: oncall saw alerts for `LogForwarderDroppedEvents` (the one Sergio added). it was firing nonstop for ~20m. cluster was also under load (runtime pods scaling).

Nadia Rahman: do we know if we’re dropping at the collector or at the sink? (loki in this env)

Caleb Johnson: looked at vector internal metrics. `vector_events_dropped_total{component_kind="sink",component_name="loki"}` increments. so it’s sink backpressure -> buffer fills -> drop.

Sergio Costa: :eyes: do we have the exact error? vector usually logs `service unavailable` / `context deadline ...

title: #eng-platform: 1746209135
preview:
Benji Okafor: Kicking off the dual-read / cutover flag design convo. We need something that avoids partial cutover states across control-plane + data-plane. Current proposal is a single boolean `kms.dual_read_enabled` + `kms.primary_key_version`. I’m worried this is too easy to mis-roll.

Ethan Park: +1. Boolean fla...
text:
Benji Okafor: Kicking off the dual-read / cutover flag design convo. We need something that avoids partial cutover states across control-plane + data-plane. Current proposal is a single boolean `kms.dual_read_enabled` + `kms.primary_key_version`. I’m worried this is too easy to mis-roll.

Ethan Park: +1. Boolean flags always end up in weird combos. If we ship this, we’ll get into a state where some regions are dual-reading and some aren’t, esp w/ config propagation delays.

Jordan Lee: What’s the failure mode we’re most worried about? If a pod is still old-only while encryption switched to new key version, it won’t be able to decrypt newly written blobs/records.

Vikram Singh: For data-plane specifically, we have both streaming requests and cached envelope keys. If we flip write before read is dual, we’ll spike decrypt failures. Seen this in other systems.

Diego Santos: I’d prefer a ...

title: #eng-platform: 1746211302
preview:
Thomas Baird: Flagging a potential footgun for Private: current hosted metrics include labels like `model`, `route`, and sometimes `tenant_id` (even if hashed). In a customer Prometheus that’s a fast path to TSDB pain. We need to decide what labels are allowed for the min pack.

Allison Grant: +1. This is exactly wh...
text:
Thomas Baird: Flagging a potential footgun for Private: current hosted metrics include labels like `model`, `route`, and sometimes `tenant_id` (even if hashed). In a customer Prometheus that’s a fast path to TSDB pain. We need to decide what labels are allowed for the min pack.

Allison Grant: +1. This is exactly what we were trying to prevent with the Private standards. For Private we need bounded cardinality at the source, not “hope Prom handles it”. What’s the current worst offender?

Nadia Rahman: From the metering side we emit `redwood_requests_total{model=...,route=...,tenant=...}` (tenant is a short hash). In hosted it’s fine bc we shard and have remote write + big TSDB, but a 3-node on-prem cluster will melt.

Logan Wright: Also note: even without tenant, `route` * x `model` explodes. Customers tend to have a lot of routes and experiment with many model variants.

Dylan Brooks...

text:
Dylan Brooks: Kicking off scope decision for Private Observability Minimum Pack dashboards. Need clear must-have vs nice-to-have for v1 so packaging + QA can lock.

Dylan Brooks: Current wireframes (WIP) here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/priv-obs-min-pack-dashboard-wireframes (see “Runtime Overview” + “Control Plane Health”).

Dylan Brooks: Please reply with (a) MUST ship in v1, (b) NICE-to-have (v1.1+), and owner. Also any panels that are “don’t ship (cardinality / too brittle)”.

Sergio Costa: For v1, I’d strongly push these as MUST:
1) Runtime Overview (golden signals + throughput) — panels: request RPS, p95/p99 latency, error rate, tokens/sec, queue depth, batch size, GPU util, GPU mem, OOM/restarts.
2) Control Plane Health — API gateway, policy/router, auth integration errors, config sync, model registry connectivity.
3) Prometheus/Grafana “Is it working” — scrape heal...

Chloe Martin: +1. also eng-platform folks will miss it if it’s in #sales.

Sean Gallagher: we should treat demo env as its own “prod-like” surface. recommendation:
- create #demo-env-alerts (or #demo-alerts)
- route alerts there
- have a user group / alias for oncall: @demo-oncall
- escalation: @sre-oncall as secondary for Sev2+ demo outages

Avery Johnson: from field side: if it hits #sales, it becomes a fire drill + 10 pings. i'd rather have a clean place + a single person to reach.

Ethan Park: agree. also migrations run in places we don’t want (non-demo envs). we already have guardrails in reset CLI but I don’t want to couple demo content to DB lifecycle.

Mina Haddad: circling back on the `recommended` alias semantics. i’m still worried we’ll accidentally create “silent upgrades” if a customer sets `quantization_profile_ref=recommended` and we treat it as a dynamic pointer.

Jordan Lee: if we store the alias, determinism is gone unless we define a specific resolution moment. and even then audit diffs get weird (config unchanged, behavior changed).

title: #eng-platform: 1740689022
preview:
Keiko Yamamoto: heads up — propose hotfix for unfair burst denials we saw in staging when telemetry is laggy. tl;dr token bucket thinks tenants are “spending” more than they are b/c usage samples arrive late/out of order → oscillation → denial spikes.

Keiko Yamamoto: plan is telemetry smoothing + stale-sample guard...
text:
Keiko Yamamoto: heads up — propose hotfix for unfair burst denials we saw in staging when telemetry is laggy. tl;dr token bucket thinks tenants are “spending” more than they are b/c usage samples arrive late/out of order → oscillation → denial spikes.

Keiko Yamamoto: plan is telemetry smoothing + stale-sample guard:
- per-tenant `observed_gpu_seconds` becomes EWMA over last N samples (window 30s, alpha=0.2)
- clamp negative deltas when samples arrive out-of-order
- if telemetry age > 20s, admission controller falls back to conservative mode: don’t decrease tokens, only refill; and emit `DENY_TELEMETRY_STALE` once global safety cap is near
- keep determinism: smoothing uses only controller-local time buckets + monotonic seq, no random

Keiko Yamamoto: PR is in progress (will be `pr-23944-hotfix-prevent-burst-starvation-under-telemetry-lag`). want quick review from @Jordan @Allison @Pe...

preview:

text:
Benji Okafor: quick review thread: redaction rules for config diffs (bot v2 + config diff API). goal is customer/tenant-safe diffs in Slack. can we align on allowlist + tests so we don’t leak anything?

Benji Okafor: current draft rules (WIP):
- allowlist specific config paths (feature flags, routing policy, quotas, model pins)
- denylist patterns for keys (token/secret/password/key/etc)
- value redaction if looks like credential (JWT-ish, PEM, long base64)
- default redact unknown keys

Benji Okafor: tagging @Kaitlyn Nguyen @Marcus Lin @Naomi Feldman for security signoff, and @Ethan Park / @Jordan Lee / @Mina Haddad for “what’s actually useful in incident triage”.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: +1 on default-redact unknown keys. if we ever do “best effort detect secrets” without default-redact, we’ll lose.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: question: are we diffing *effective* config or *declared* config? effective...

text:
Connor O'Brien: we need to get ahead of rollout dashboards for RBAC v2 flags before we start canarying in prod. can we align on *what* we’re measuring + who owns panels/alerts?

Connor O'Brien: main flags in play (as of current branch):
- `rbac_v2.dual_read` (evaluate v1 + v2, enforce v1, emit compare metrics)
- `rbac_v2.enforce_gateway`
- `rbac_v2.enforce_services`
- `rbac_v2.debug_denial_headers` (admin-only)

Connor O'Brien: goals for dashboards: deny rate, latency impact, mismatch rate (v1 vs v2), and “coverage gaps” (unannotated endpoints / unknown permissions). need views per env (staging/prod) + per deployment mode (Hosted/Dedicated/Private).

Logan Wright: +1. I can own the metrics plumbing / grafana dashboard skeleton. need names/labels nailed down so we don’t end up with 12 almost-identical queries.

Allison Grant: for alerts, we should keep it minimal but high-signal. deny ...

title: #eng-platform: 1740215540
preview:
Ethan Park: Heads up: we’re about to land the readiness gate before nodes can join serving pools (PR 18496). Want to sanity check fallback behavior when capacity is tight. If gate is strict, a bad validation run could block scale-up and we’d be “healthy but empty”. How do we want this to behave?

Benji Okafor: Curre...
text:
Ethan Park: Heads up: we’re about to land the readiness gate before nodes can join serving pools (PR 18496). Want to sanity check fallback behavior when capacity is tight. If gate is strict, a bad validation run could block scale-up and we’d be “healthy but empty”. How do we want this to behave?

Benji Okafor: Current implementation is strict by default: node must be `Ready` in readiness service or it won’t get added to serving pool (and stays cordoned). There’s a feature flag to disable the gate per region/pool as a kill switch.

Jordan Lee: +1 strict by default. We have too many post-provision surprises. Let’s not make “kinda-valid” a thing.

Vanessa Ortiz: Strict is correct, but we need a clear escape hatch during incidents. If us-east A100 is short, ops will ask “can we serve on nodes stuck in Validating?”

Keiko Yamamoto: From autoscaling POV: if nodes aren’t admitted, the pool l...

title: #eng-platform: 1740112830
preview:
Jordan Lee: Kicking off a thread on rollout policy guardrail edge cases. We keep running into ambiguities in reviews: (1) step size vs approval gates, (2) soak time overrides, (3) admin roles / who can bypass. Want to align before we lock policy engine semantics for GA.

Paula Mendes: +1. This is the stuff customers...
text:
Jordan Lee: Kicking off a thread on rollout policy guardrail edge cases. We keep running into ambiguities in reviews: (1) step size vs approval gates, (2) soak time overrides, (3) admin roles / who can bypass. Want to align before we lock policy engine semantics for GA.

Paula Mendes: +1. This is the stuff customers will hit immediately. For Hosted GA I want the Console to have predictable errors + a path forward (not just “denied”).

Benji Okafor: Starting list of edge cases:
- If max_step_percent=10 and you have approvals required at >=25%, can you jump 10->25 (15pt jump) with approval? or still blocked?
- If min_soak=30m, can SRE/owner force-progress early (like 5m) if metrics are clean?
- Who is “admin”? tenant_admin vs org_owner vs rollout_approver.

Ethan Park: Also: what happens when policy changes mid-rollout. E.g. admin tightens max_step from 25->5 while rollout is paused at ...

title: #eng-platform: 1740082033
preview:
Cole Summers: Heads up: while wiring burst metering -> billing pipeline, I’m seeing a few edge cases around retries/idempotency that we should nail down before we tell Finance “numbers are clean”.

Cole Summers: Current plan (per PRD) is we emit `BurstUsageEvent` per request w/ fields: tenant_id, region, hw_class, m...
text:
Cole Summers: Heads up: while wiring burst metering -> billing pipeline, I’m seeing a few edge cases around retries/idempotency that we should nail down before we tell Finance “numbers are clean”.

Cole Summers: Current plan (per PRD) is we emit `BurstUsageEvent` per request w/ fields: tenant_id, region, hw_class, model, burst_gpu_ms, tokens_out, tokens_in, admitted=true/false, denial_reason. The retry problem: gateway retries on 5xx/timeout and we can double-count if we emit on both attempts.

Nadia Rahman: +1. Billing pipeline today assumes events are append-only and unique by event_id. If we can’t guarantee uniqueness, reconciliation gets ugly fast.

Ethan Park: Can we rely on existing request ids from gateway? We have `x-redwood-request-id` (uuid) + optional `x-idempotency-key` (customer supplied) in Hosted. Dedicated gateway also sets a stable request id per attempt but I don’t t...

title: #eng-platform: 1739982105
preview:
Ethan Park: looping in @Naomi Feldman + @Marcus Lin on RBAC/audit for the route-level quantization toggle. We need to lock down who can enable profiles + what we log for diffs.

Benji Okafor: +1. Control plane endpoint is basically `PATCH /v1/routes/{id}` w/ `quantization_profile_ref` + `quantization_enabled`. Today...
text:
Ethan Park: looping in @Naomi Feldman + @Marcus Lin on RBAC/audit for the route-level quantization toggle. We need to lock down who can enable profiles + what we log for diffs.

Benji Okafor: +1. Control plane endpoint is basically `PATCH /v1/routes/{id}` w/ `quantization_profile_ref` + `quantization_enabled`. Today route updates require `route:write` which maps to Org Admin + Project Admin. Question: is that sufficient for beta?

Naomi Feldman: for beta, we can keep it simple but we need to be explicit. For enterprise security reviews, they’ll ask:
- who can change it (roles)
- is there an approval workflow
- can they export an audit trail w/ before/after

Marcus Lin: also needs to cover kill-switch actions. If SRE flips global disable, that should produce an audit event too.

Chloe Martin: Console side: design has “Enable quantization profile” toggle on Route Settings. We can hide i...

text:
Jordan Lee: quick q on authz decision cache TTLs for the policy-evaluator client. what are we comfortable with for v2? (esp w/ deny-by-default + audits)

Kaitlyn Nguyen: depends what we're caching. if it's “allow/deny for (subject, action, scope)” we need to be really careful w/ role change propagation.

Ines Carvalho: +1. scopes can be org/project/env/cluster. role bindings change at org scope but decision is per-scope. invalidation has to understand hierarchy.

Jordan Lee: current PR draft (client-side cache) is essentially:
- key: subject_id + action + scope_key + policy_bundle_version
- value: decision + reason
- ttl: ???
- negative caching for denies: also ???

Amara Diallo: gateway side: would prefer short TTL for allows, and maybe even shorter for denies? but we also don’t want to hammer evaluator on every request.

Logan Wright: from telemetry side, we can instrument:
- cache_...

text:
Priya Natarajan: ok last thread before we call beta go/no-go today. pls drop *any* last-minute review questions/unknowns here (security, audit, SRE thresholds, UX copy). I’ll assign owners + due times.

Ethan Park: q1: what’s our explicit behavior when telemetry is missing/stale during gate eval? I remember “fail-closed unless manual override” but want it written + reflected in API responses.

Jordan Lee: +1. policy engine currently treats “unknown” as FAIL for automated promote, but doesn’t auto-abort. it sets gate_status=unknown and blocks promotion unless `force_promote`.

Priya Natarajan: owner? Jordan can you summarize final semantics in 3 bullets + where it’s implemented (policy engine vs control plane)? need for release notes.

Jordan Lee: yup, I’ll post by 11:30.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: security q: RBAC for force-promote/hold/abort. do we have a distinct permission or is it bundled u...

Talia Benson: ok folks, need to lock beta customer selection + enablement plan for Dedicated staged rollouts. aiming to flip flags next week (post release train), but we need:
1) who are the 1-2 design partners (account + pool ids)
2) exact feature flags + scope (account vs pool vs region)
3) support readiness (runbook link, oncall routing, comms owner)

can we converge today? :eyes:

Lauren Bishop: proposed design partners:
- Lattice AI (Dedicated, high change rate, asked for safer upgrades)
- EmberSearch (Dedicated, throughput heavy, wants "no downtime" model bumps)

HarborBank feels like "beta++" given audit/change mgmt. I’d rather bring them in once audit export fix is confirmed + we’ve seen 1-2 clean rollouts.

Liam O'Rourke: +1. Lattice AI TAM is ready to schedule a working session. EmberSearch as well. HarborBank already asked about audit exports + retention; they will get stuc...

text:
Ethan Park: quick q before we lock the readiness APIs — who should be allowed to mutate node readiness state vs read-only? also need a plan for audit events when image channel/version changes for a pool.

Benji Okafor: +1. current proto has:
- `GET /v1/fleet/nodes/{node_id}/readiness`
- `POST /v1/fleet/nodes/{node_id}/events` (validation results)
- `PATCH /v1/fleet/nodes/{node_id}/readiness` (state transition)
Right now auth is just “internal service token”. needs tightening.

Jordan Lee: for state machine integrity: only ONE writer should be authoritative for `PATCH readiness`.
If multiple actors can set `Ready/Quarantined` directly, we’ll end up in weird transitions.

Ethan Park: agree. thinking:
- provisioning orchestrator is the only caller that can advance Allocated->Provisioning->Validating->Ready (and quarantine on policy)
- validation agent can only submit validation artifacts...

title: #eng-platform: 1739473300
preview:
Allison Grant: heads up: for Private upgrade + rollback work, we need to finalize audit log events for the upgrade lifecycle. Compliance customers (esp. on-prem) are asking what we emit + required fields. Want to lock schema this week so installer + CP can align.

Kevin Osei: +1. For SIEM integrations, they keep ask...
text:
Allison Grant: heads up: for Private upgrade + rollback work, we need to finalize audit log events for the upgrade lifecycle. Compliance customers (esp. on-prem) are asking what we emit + required fields. Want to lock schema this week so installer + CP can align.

Kevin Osei: +1. For SIEM integrations, they keep asking for “who/what/when/where + outcome” + correlation ids. Also need a stable event name/version so parsers don’t break.

Irene Choi: question: are we emitting from the control plane only, or also directly from the installer CLI (since on-prem can be disconnected)?

Ethan Park: both, unfortunately. Control plane can emit canonical events for VPC deployments. For air-gapped/on-prem, installer must write locally + optionally forward to customer’s syslog/SIEM endpoint. We should keep payloads identical.

Naomi Feldman: +1 to identical payloads. Also: explicit guidance on redac...

title: #eng-platform: 1739408123
preview:
Amara Diallo: quick q for RBAC v2: where do we want to enforce authz checks long-term — gateway vs service? I’m leaning gateway because consistency + perf, but worried about bypass/internal calls. Looking for a decision we can turn into an ADR.

Jordan Lee: tl;dr IMO service must be source of truth. Gateway-only is ...
text:
Amara Diallo: quick q for RBAC v2: where do we want to enforce authz checks long-term — gateway vs service? I’m leaning gateway because consistency + perf, but worried about bypass/internal calls. Looking for a decision we can turn into an ADR.

Jordan Lee: tl;dr IMO service must be source of truth. Gateway-only is too easy to bypass (internal service-to-service, console talking directly in some flows, private installer hitting CP). Gateway can be defense-in-depth / early reject.

Ethan Park: +1. Control plane endpoints need checks in-process. We already have internal jobs calling the same handlers w/o going thru gateway.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: also authn context differs. Gateway sees external identity, but service has to validate org/project/env scope anyway. If we push checks to gateway only, services will start trusting headers and that’s… spooky.

Amara Diallo: yup, the “trust an X-Auth...

title: #eng-platform: 1739402017

2) do we auto-timeout holds?
3) what does “resume” do to gate windows / samples?

Ethan Park: +1. Control plane wi...
text:
Jordan Lee: Need to nail down HOLD semantics for Dedicated staged rollouts before we lock the state machine. Questions:
1) what does hold *actually* freeze (routing? gate eval? auto-promote/auto-abort?)
2) do we auto-timeout holds?
3) what does “resume” do to gate windows / samples?

Ethan Park: +1. Control plane wise we can represent `state=HELD` with metadata (who/when/reason). But we need deterministic behavior across policy engine + routing.

Rafael Mendes: From SRE side: hold should mean “stop changing things” BUT we still need an escape hatch if the canary is melting prod. I’m worried about a hold blocking auto-abort.

Benji Okafor: Current implementation sketch (not merged yet):
- routing stays pinned to current stage partition while held
- controller stops promotions
- gate evaluator still computes pass/fail/unknown
But unclear whether we *act* on failures while held.

Jordan ...

Ethan Park: circling back on the version compatibility policy for Private upgrades. we need to decide where the matrix lives + how it's kept current. also where we enforce (installer vs control plane vs terraform).

Quentin Harris: +1. i can own the matrix but we need a home w/ PR review + release process. right now it’s scattered between docs + tribal knowledge.

Benji Okafor: proposing: matrix lives in main `redwood` repo (not installer), so it’s tied to control plane releases. installer consumes it (pinned by target version) and blocks unsupported paths.

Connor O'Brien: i like it in `redwood` repo for release-train reasons. but installer is what operators run. we need to guarantee the installer has the policy for air-gapped too.

Quentin Harris: we can do both: canonical source in `redwood` repo, and publish it as an artifact alongside Private release bundle.

title: #eng-platform: 1739389805
preview:

Ethan Park: yep. proposal from control plane side:
- tenant config field `dedicated.burst.ena...
text:
Talia Benson: need to lock down rollout gating for Dedicated burst before we hand this to release eng. can someone confirm the plan for (1) per-tenant enablement, (2) per-region/hw allow list, (3) emergency rollback switch?
Ethan Park: yep. proposal from control plane side:
- tenant config field `dedicated.burst.enabled` (default false)
- tenant config `dedicated.burst.max_ratio`, `...cooldown_s`, `...tier`
- global gate: `burst_global_enabled` (feature flag) so we can hard-disable without touching tenant configs
- allowlist gate: region+hw-class list (ex: us-east/H100, eu-west/L40S)
Benji Okafor: implementation detail: I was going to put allowlist under the policy engine config rather than LaunchDarkly so it’s auditable and versioned. global kill switch in LD (fast toggle).
Jordan Lee: +1. we need determinism for admission decisions. if region/hw allowlist is in LD, we risk inconsist...
